channel.title: Cemetery on Berrytree.org
channel.description: Recent content in Cemetery on Berrytree.org

item.title: Cora Berry McVicker
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Cora Berry McVicker
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, NC → Georgia
Gen 4Robert Berry × Elizabeth Everett — NC and Georgia
Gen 5Jesse Lee Berry × Nancy Henry — Etowah, Alabama → Muscogee, Georgia
Gen 6Cora Mae Berry × James S. McVicker — Alabama and Georgia

Generation 6 Cora Mae Berry McVicker

Born
Alabama
Died
Georgia
Married
James S. McVicker
Parents
Jesse Lee Berry and Nancy Henry Berry, Alabama
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Cora Berry McVicker#

L to R. Sarah Florence Berry Kelly, William Oscar Berry, Albert Edgar Berry, Addie Lena Berry Maners, Cora Mae Berry McVickers, Walter Glenn Berry is not shown in this picture 1900 Etowah, Alabama Census

item.title: David Middleton Berry
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. David Middleton Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Robert Berry Jr. × Mary Waggoner — NC and Fayette County, Alabama
Gen 4David Middleton Berry × Didama Box / Frances Henderson — Orange County, NC → Fayette County, Alabama

Generation 4 David Middleton Berry

Born
April 14, 1805, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
May 7, 1880, Fayette County, Alabama
Married
1st: Didama Box (married c. 1826, died December 14, 1856) — 2nd: Frances E. Henderson (married April 15, 1857, died May 17, 1885)
Parents
Robert Berry Jr. and Mary Waggoner Berry, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of David Middleton Berry#

David Middleton Berry was born April 14th, 1805 in Orange County, North Carolina. He died on May 7th, 1880. David married Didama Box about 1826. Didama Box was born in South Carolina on February 14th, 1807. She died on December 14th, 1856 in Fayette County, Alabama. David Middleton Berry married his second wife, Frances E. Henderson on April 15, 1857 in Fayette County, Alabama. Frances was born on January 10th, 1818 in South Carolina and died May 17th, 1885.

item.title: Henry Bradford Berry
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Henry Bradford Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Robert Berry Jr. × Mary Waggoner — NC and Fayette County, Alabama
Gen 4Thompson Berry × Rosanah Gutman — Fayette County, Alabama
Gen 5Henry Bradford Berry × Malissa Jane Jeffries — Fayette County, Alabama

Generation 5 Henry Bradford Berry

Born
December 6, 1829, Fayette County, Alabama
Died
April 22, 1895, Fayette County, Alabama
Married
Malissa Jane Jeffries (married June 4, 1874, died October 6, 1921)
Parents
Thompson Berry and Rosanah Gutman Berry, Fayette County, Alabama
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Henry Bradford Berry#

Henry Bradford Berry was born Dec 6, 1829 in Fayette County, Alabama. He married Malissa Jane Jeffries on June 4, 1874. Malissa was born Nov 6, 1851 and died on October 6, 1921. Her headstone is pictured on the right. Henry Bradford Berry’s headstone is on the left. Henry Bradford died on Apr 22, 1895. Henry and Malissa, along with his parents, Thompson and Rosanah were buried in Pleasant Hill Methodist Church Cemetery in Fayette County, Alabama.

item.title: John Robert Berry
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. John Robert Berry
John Robert Berry

Generation 5 John Robert Berry

Born
September 25, 1842, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
October 10, 1917, Orange County, North Carolina
Married
Elizabeth Frances Bowling — married November 30, 1865
Parents
Thomas Person Berry and Sarah Lunsford, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline
Direct Berry Line
  1. Robert and Mary Williamson Berry — Princess Anne County, Virginia
  2. Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry — Orange County, North Carolina
  3. William Berry and Hannah Cate — Orange County, NC
  4. Thomas Person Berry and Sarah Lunsford — Orange County, NC
  5. John Robert Berry and Elizabeth Frances Bowling — Orange County, NC

Early Life#

John Robert Berry was born on September 25, 1842, in Orange County, North Carolina. He was the third son of Thomas Person and Sarah Lunsford Berry. There is not a lot known about the period between Thomas Person Berry and Sarah Lunsford’s marriage on September 28, 1831, and the birth of their first son in 1836.

item.title: Kate Berry
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Kate Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Joshua Berry × Nancy Ellison — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 4Lewis Berry × Sally Toler — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 5Ira Berry × Nancy Wynn — NC and Kentucky
Gen 6Franklin Pierce Berry × Sophie Sellers — Kentucky
Gen 7Kate Berry × Benjamin Franklin Moake — Kentucky → Williamson County, Illinois

Generation 7 Kate Berry

Born
August 12, 1896, Sebree, Webster County, Kentucky
Died
August 22, 1966, Williamson County, Illinois
Married
Benjamin Franklin Moake (born August 18, 1892, died February 9, 1979)
Parents
Franklin Pierce Berry and Sophia M. Sellers Berry, Kentucky
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Kate Berry#

Kate Berry Was born Aug 12, 1896 Sebree, Webster Co., Ky, she died Aug 22, 1966 and is buried at Lake Creek Cemetery, Williamson Co., Illinois. Kate married Benjamin Franklin Moake in Williamson Co, Illinois, son of Eziekel Moake and Dell Everett. He was born August 18, 1892 in Lake Creek, Williamson Co., Illinois, and died February 09, 1979 in Williamson Co., Illinois. In 1941, Kate had her birth certificate replaced, the witnesses for her were A.M. Sellers, an uncle age 75, Samuel Hansford husband of Rebecca Sellers and G.W. Bronson, a non relative age 80 years. The certificate is listed as #175914 in the bureau of vital statistics, Commonwealth of Kentucky, Webster Co.

item.title: Joshua Berry Jr. Ohio
item.description:
Joshua Berry Jr. (1801–1879)

Son of Joshua and Nancy Ellison Berry, migrated from Orange County, NC, to Ohio, Indiana, and Iowa.

Joshua Berry Jr., Son of Joshua Berry and Nancy Ellison Berry#

Ancestors:

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County, Virginia

item.title: Robert Berry (OC)
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Robert Berry (OC)
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina

Generation 2 Robert Berry

Born
c. 1729, Princess Anne County, Virginia
Died
1814, Orange County, North Carolina
Married
Elizabeth Cate — married c. 1759, Orange County, NC
Parents
Robert Berry and Mary Williamson, Princess Anne County, VA
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Robert Berry#

Robert Berry was the son of Robert Berry and Mary Williamson of Princess Anne County, Virginia, and he came to Orange County, North Carolina, in 1751.

item.title: Elizabeth Cate Berry
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Elizabeth Cate Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina

Generation 2 (by marriage) Elizabeth Cate Berry

Born
c. 1735, Virginia
Died
c. 1817, Orange County, North Carolina
Married
Robert Berry — married c. 1759, Orange County, NC
Parents
John and Margery Lawrence Cate, Prince George County, Virginia
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Elizabeth Cate Berry#

Elizabeth Cate was the daughter of John and Margery Cate. Her oldest brother was Joshua Cate and her youngest brother was Thomas Cate, who was a chain carrier when Robert Berry’s Granville Grant was surveyed in 1753. Robert Berry and Elizabeth Cate were married about 1759. At least three of their children were already married before marriage records were kept in Hillsborough. The ages of their children would indicate that their marriage took place around 1759.

item.title: Hannah Cate Berry
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Hannah Cate Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3William Berry × Hannah Cate — Orange County, North Carolina

Generation 3 (by marriage) Hannah Cate Berry

Born
1770, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
1858, Orange County, North Carolina
Married
William Berry — married August 12, 1799, Orange County, NC
Parents
William and Elizabeth Smith (Messersmith) Cate
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Hannah Cate Berry#

Hannah Cate was born in 1770 and she died in 1858 in Orange County, NC (1810 and 1850 Orange County Censuses; William Cate’s will, dated November 10, 1775, confirms children). Hannah Cate was the daughter of William and Elizabeth Smith (Messersmith) Cate.

item.title: Sarah Cate Berry Pigg
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Sarah Cate Berry Pigg
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Thomas Berry × Sarah Cate — Orange County, NC → Wayne County, TN

Generation 3 (by marriage) Sarah Cate Berry Pigg

Born
December 27, 1782, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
March 7, 1875, Wayne County, Tennessee
Married
1st: Thomas Berry — married August 11, 1800, Orange County, NC · 2nd: John Pigg — married December 11, 1815, Williamson County, TN
Parents
William Cate and Elizabeth Messersmith Cate
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Sarah Cate#

Sarah Cate was the daughter of William and Elizabeth Messersmith Cate and was born in North Carolina on December 27, 1782. Nothing is known about Sarah’s early life except that she had two brothers and two sisters. The brothers were John Cate and Benjamin Cate and her two sisters were Charity and Hannah Cate.

item.title: William Clarence Berry NC, ALA, TX
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. William Clarence Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3William Berry × Hannah Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 4William Clarence Berry × Sally Bowles / Massa Ann Tompkins — Orange County, NC → Fayette County, AL → Burnet County, TX

Generation 4 William Clarence Berry

Born
October 17, 1803, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
September 8, 1878, Burnet County, Texas
Married
1st: Sally Bowles (married November 20, 1826; died 1833) — 2nd: Massa Ann Tompkins (married 1836)
Parents
William Berry and Hannah Cate Berry, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

William Clarence Berry, son of William and Hannah Cate Berry#

William Clarence Berry was born on October 17th 1803 in Orange County, North Carolina and died on 08 September 1878 in Burnet County Texas. He and his two brothers were listed on Hannah Cate Berry’s 1810 census. The family was living in the old Patrick Rutherford plantation house which Robert Berry bought in 1787. They may have lived there for many years but Hannah did not list in the 1820 Orange County, NC, census.

June 1, 1808, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
April 30, 1884, Orange County, North Carolina
Married

Parents
William Berry and Hannah Cate, Orange County, NC

The Story of Thomas Person Berry#

Thomas Person Berry

item.title: Aldridge Berry Farquhar Waggoner Connection
item.description:
The Aldridge, Berry, Farquhar & Waggoner Connection

Four families intermarried across multiple generations from Orange County, NC, to Fayette County, Alabama, and Burnet County, Texas.

There are several marriages in different Berry lines involving these four families.

From Henry Waggoners 1784 Will (Orange County NC Deed Book A Page 231) Henry and Ketriana Waggoner’s Children:

item.title: John H. Berry ALA TX
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. John H. Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Robert Berry Jr. × Mary Waggoner — NC and Fayette County, Alabama

Generation 5 John H. Berry

Born
September 29, 1827, Fayette County, Alabama
Died
October 7, 1904, Burnet County, Texas
Married
Eliza Catherine Dennis — married July 1848, Fayette County, AL
Parents
David Middleton Berry and Didama Box Berry
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

Lineage#

  1. Gen 1 — Robert and Mary Williamson Berry — Princess Anne County, Virginia
  2. Gen 2 — Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry — Orange County, North Carolina
  3. Gen 3 — Robert Jr. and Mary Waggoner Berry — NC and Fayette County, Alabama
  4. Gen 4 — David Middleton and Didama Box Berry — Fayette County, Alabama
  5. Gen 5 — John H. and Catherine Dennis Berry — Alabama and Texas

John H. Berry born September 29, 1827, Fayette Co., AL, m. July 1848 in Fayette Co., AL, Eliza Catherine Dennis, b. January 21, 1828, AL (daughter of Benjamin Dennis and Elizabeth Berry), d. July 21, 1913, bur. Cauble Cemetery, Burnet Co., TX. John died October 7, 1904, Burnet Co., TX, bur. Cauble Cemetery.

item.title: Mattie Lee Strickland Ala
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Mattie Lee Berry Strickland
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Robert Berry Jr. × Mary Waggoner — NC and Fayette County, Alabama

Generation 6 Mattie Lee Berry Strickland

Born
1882, Fayette County, Alabama
Died
1916, Berry Cemetery, Fayette County, Alabama
Married
David Loyd Strickland — married c. 1902
Parents
Henry Bradford Berry and Malissa Jeffries
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

Lineage#

  1. Gen 1 — Robert and Mary Williamson Berry — Princess Anne County, Virginia
  2. Gen 2 — Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry — Orange County, North Carolina
  3. Gen 3 — Robert Jr. and Mary Waggoner Berry — NC and Fayette County, Alabama
  4. Gen 4 — Thompson and Roseann Gutman Berry — Fayette County, Alabama
  5. Gen 5 — Henry Bradford and Malissa Jeffries Berry

The Story of Mattie Lee Berry#

Mattie Lee Berry was born 1882 in Fayette County, Alabama. She married David Loyd Strickland about 1902. In 1910 Mattie Lee Berry Strickland and her family were living with her widowed mother Malissa Jeffries Berry in Holly Springs, Fayette County, Alabama.

item.title: Ada Lee Berry NC
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Ada Lee Ann Berry Rogers

Gen 5John Robert Berry × Elizabeth Frances Bowling — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 6Ada Lee Ann Berry × William Jackson Rogers — Orange County, NC → Person County, NC

Generation 6 Ada Lee Ann Berry Rogers

Born
July 11, 1875
Died
November 22, 1968
Married
William Jackson Rogers (married September 1, 1895)
Parents
John Robert Berry and Elizabeth Frances Bowling Berry, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

Ada Lee Ann Berry Rogers#

Ada Lee Berry was born July 11th 1875. On Sept 1, 1895 she married William Jackson Rogers. William J. Rogers was born in Granville County, NC on October 10th 1864. He was the son

of William Clinton Rogers and Rachel Starke Holeman.

item.title: Addie Lena Berry
item.description:
Ancestors:

  5. Jesse Lee and Nancy Ann Henry Berry, Georgia and Alabama
  6. Addie Lena Berry and Hampton Fantom Manners, Spaulding GA & Guilford County, North Carolina.

Addie Lena Berry and her siblings

item.title: Albert Edgar Berry
item.description:
Albert Edgar Berry, son of Jesse Lee and Nancy Ann Henry Berry#

  • Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
  • Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
  • Gen 3 David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, NC → Georgia
  • Gen 4 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Everett — Georgia
  • Gen 5 Jesse Lee Berry × Nancy Ann Henry — Georgia & Alabama
  • Gen 6 Albert Edgar Berry × Ella Claudia Dorton — Georgia

**L to R. Sarah Florence Berry Kelly, William Oscar Berry, Albert Edgar Berry, Addie Lena Berry Maners, Cora Mae Berry McVickers, Walter Glenn Berry is not shown in this picture Albert Edgar Berry **was born on December 28, 1881 in Etowah County Alabama. Albert died om May 23, 1964. He and his first wife are buried at Bethany Baptist Church Cemetery, Henry County, Georgia. Albert Edgar Berry and Ella Claudia Dorton were married on the 23 January 1905. She was born February 2, 1880 and died February 15 1916.

item.title: Alexander Howard Berry Tx
item.description:
Alexander Howard Berry, son of John Berry and Mary Howell Berry#

Ancestors:#

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry
  3. Generation Robert Jr and Mary Camp Berry
  4. Generation George and Susan Berry
  5. Generation John and Mary Howell Berry

Alexander Howard Berry was born on August 24th 1854 in New Danville, Rusk County Texas.

item.title: Basil Manley Berry Ga
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Basil Manley Berry Ga
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, NC → Georgia
Gen 4Robert Berry × Elizabeth Everitt — Milton County, Georgia
Gen 5Basil Manley Berry × Mary Elizabeth Watson — Milton County, Georgia

Generation 5 Basil Manley Berry

Born
June 19, 1856, Milton County, Georgia
Died
October 1, 1914, Roswell, Fulton County, Georgia
Married
Mary Elizabeth Watson (married 1878; born June 24, 1855; died September 13, 1941)
Parents
Robert and Elizabeth Everitt Berry
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Basil Manley Berry#

Basil Manley Berry born June 19th, 1856 in Milton County, Georgia. In 1878 at the age of 20 he married Mary Elizabeth Watson born June 24, 1855, daughter of William L. Watson and Elizabeth A. ? Watson. Basil Manley Berry died Oct 1, 1914 in Roswell, Fulton County, Georgia and Mary Elizabeth Berry died Sept 13, 1941 in Roswell, Fulton County, Georgia. They are buried at Willeo Cemetery in Cobb County, Georgia.

The Story of Cora Dena Berry#

Cora Dena Berry

On June 26th 1889 she married Asahel Frank Breeze. This Couple lived in Orange County, North Carolina and had nine children.

item.title: Dora Ellen Turley Ga
item.description:
Dora Ellen Turley, daughter of Nathan and Mary Jori Berry Turley#

Ancestors#

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry
  3. Generation David J. and Mary Blalock Berry
  4. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Everitt Berry Berry
  5. Generation Mary Jori Berry Turley and Nathan Turley

DORA ELLEN TURLEY born April 11, 1874 in Milton County Georgia and died June 6, 1915 after giving birth to twin daughters. She married George Robert Pace before 1892. Dora Ellen Turley Pace is buried in the Holly Springs Cemetery, Cobb County Georgia.

1881, Georgia
Died
1961
Married

Robert Riley Berry and Martha 'Mattie' Starke Berry

Siblings of Enoch Meril Berry

Census Information

1930 United States Federal Census, Vinemont, Cullman Alabama#

  • Edna Berry, age 48

  • Louis Berry, age 14
  • Buel Berry, age 11
  • Winifred Berry, age 9 (Shorty)

L-R: Chester Berry, Enoch Merrill Berry, Winfred (Shorty) Berry, Lewis Berry, Buel Berry, Mary Edna Stuart Berry, Claude Berry, Ester Berry.

item.title: Fiddleton Cemetery
item.description:
Fiddleton Cemetery Visitation — July 21, 2007

The Berry family burial ground in Orange County, NC, final resting place of Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry.

The Fiddleton Cemetery was the site of a historic family gathering on July 21, 2007, the first visit to our ancestors’ graves for many of the descendants. They came from 14 states and from as far away as California. The photograph at the top of the page was taken that day at the Robert Berry Cemetery.

item.title: Frederick M. Berry Iowa
item.description:
Frederick M. Berry (1874–1951)

Son of Harrison and Henrietta Havens Berry, Linn County, Iowa, to Polk County, Wisconsin.

Frederick M. Berry, son of Harrison and Henrietta Havens Berry#

Ancestors#

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County, Virginia.
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.
  3. Generation Joshua and Nancy Ellison Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.
  4. Generation Joshua Berry Jr. and Anna Thorpe Berry: Ohio
  5. Generation Lewis and Elizabeth Haywood Berry: Indiana and Kansas
  6. Generation Harrison and Henrietta Havens Berry: Iowa and Colorado

FREDERICK M. BERRY was born 28 February 1874 in Palo, Linn County, Iowa to Harrison and Henrietta (Havens) Berry. When Fred was four years old in 1878, his mother Henrietta died. According to the newspaper article in the Vinton Eagle: “Her husband was cutting a large tree down close to where they lived, when Henrietta attempted to go near where he was to tell him that she was going to a neighbors, having her babe in her arms and a little girl running along with her, when the tree fell, crushing her almost into a jelly, while remarkable as it may seem, the baby still lives, though probably fatally injured. The little girl too, narrowly escaped.” RosaBelle did survive, but Henrietta died instantly and baby Eliza died within three days.

item.title: George Rufus Berry NC
item.description:
George Rufus Berry (1829–1909)

Son of William Clarence Berry and Sally Bowles, from Orange County, NC, to Fayette County, Alabama, and Burnet County, Texas.

George Rufus Berry, son of William Clarence Berry and Sally Bowles Berry#

  • Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
  • Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
  • Gen 3 William Berry × Hannah Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
  • Gen 4 William Clarence Berry × Sally Bowles — Orange County, North Carolina

George Rufus Berry was born on February 29th 1829 in Orange County, North Carolina. George Rufus Berry was only about 6 years old when his mother died and his father decided to remove the family to Fayette County, Alabama where other Berry Family members were already settled. George’s sister Elizabeth Jane was only 4 years old and the baby, Roan Frances Berry was just 2.

item.title: Harrison Berry Iowa
item.description:
Harrison Berry (1854–1934)

Son of Lewis and Elizabeth Haywood Berry, Benton County, Iowa, to Pueblo, Colorado.

Harrison Berry, Son Of Lewis And Elizabeth Haywood Berry#

Ancestors#

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County, Virginia.
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.
  3. Generation Joshua and Nancy Ellison Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.
  4. Generation Joshua Berry Jr. and Anna Thorpe Berry: Ohio
  5. Generation Lewis and Elizabeth Haywood Berry: Indiana and Kansas

Harrison Berry was born 31 May 1854 in Benton County, Iowa to Lewis and Eliza (Hayward) Berry. He was named after his uncle Harrison Berry, the son of Joshua and Anna (Thorpe) Berry, the brother of his father Lewis. He was the second child born to Lewis and Eliza, who were united in marriage on 18 March 1851 in Boone County, Indiana. Lewis and Eliza, along with Lewis’s parents Joshua and Anna (Thorpe) Berry and Eliza’s father [Abijah Hayward] had moved from Indiana to Iowa prior to the birth of Harrison. They all engaged in farming in the Benton and Linn County, Iowa area.

item.title: James Berry Tenn
item.description:
James Berry, son of Joshua Berry and Nancy Ellison Berry#

  • Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
  • Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
  • Gen 3 Joshua Berry × Nancy Ellison — Orange County, North Carolina

James Berry was born in 1800 in Orange County, North Carolina. James Berry and Mary Parker were married in North Carolina about 1829. Their first three daughters were born in North Carolina. The oldest daughter, Nancy, was born in 1830. The youngest daughter, Delila, was born in North Carolina in 1836 and was 14 years old in the 1850 Smith County, Tennessee census. The oldest child born in Tennessee was, Martha, who was 11 years old in 1850. This means James and Mary were in North Carolina in 1836 and in Tennessee in 1839. I have no absolute proof that the James Berry on this page is related to us but all the facts indicate he was..

Robert Berry’s Deed to Joshua Berry with James Berry as a witness. Dated February 24 1811 (Orange County Deed Book 14 Page 57)

item.title: James P. Berry NC
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. James P. Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3William Berry × Hannah Cate — Orange County, NC
Gen 4Thomas Person Berry × Sarah Lunsford — Orange County, NC
Gen 5James P. Berry × Artillia Wedding — Orange County, NC

Generation 5 James P. Berry

Born
1845, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
February 15, 1912, Orange County, North Carolina
Married
Artillia Wedding (married September 11, 1871)
Parents
Thomas Person Berry and Sarah Lunsford, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of James P. Berry#

James P. Berry was born in 1845 in Orange County, North Carolina. On September 11 1871 James P. Berry married Artillia Wedding who was born about 1851. Artillia is the daughter of Joseph and Louisa Burton Wedding. In the 1850 Census Joseph Wedding listed house # 1736 and

James P. Berry age 8 was living with his father in the house listing # 1747. James and Artillia Berry had six children. Two of them died young.

item.title: James Thomas Wilson NC
item.description:
James Thomas Wilson (1857–1929)

Father-in-law of Pattie Swiney Berry, Orange County, North Carolina.

James Thomas Wilson, father-in-law of Pattie Swiney Berry#

JAMES THOMAS WILSON was born in January 1857 in North Carolina. He died in 1929 and is buried at Little River Presbyterian Church Cemetery, Little River Township, Orange County, NC.

item.title: Jesse Lee Berry
item.description:
Jesse Lee Berry, son of Robert and Elizabeth Everett Berry#

  • Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
  • Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
  • Gen 3 David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, NC → Georgia
  • Gen 4 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Everett — North Carolina & Georgia

JESSE LEE BERRY was born on December 2, 1852 in Roswell, Cobb County Georgia. Jesse Lee married Nancy Ann Henry who was born December 30 1857 in Ellenwood, Clayton Co., Georgia. They were married December 16 1874 in Clayton County Georgia. Nancy was the daughter of Edward Zachary “Zack” Henry and Mary Ann Mitchell. Zack’s Father was Jesse G. Henry and Nancy Hammock. Mary Ann’s father was Thomas Mitchell, son of Hinchey Mitchell and Elizabeth Ruff (daughter of William Ruff and Rebecca). In the picture of Jesse Lee on the right there seems to be a faded date of July 1875 written on the picture. He would have been 22 Yrs 7 Months old.

item.title: Joshua Berry Jr. Ohio
item.description:
Joshua Berry Jr. — Ohio Detail

Biography and Census Records, Orange County NC to Benton County Iowa

Joshua Berry Jr., Son Of Joshua Berry And Nancy Ellison Berry#

ANCESTORS:

  • Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County, Virginia

  • Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.

item.title: Leona Berry Borden (TX)
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Leona Berry Borden
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Robert Berry Jr. × Mary Waggoner — Orange County, NC → Fayette County, AL
Gen 4George Berry × Susan Berry — NC → Fayette County, AL
Gen 5John Berry × Mary Howell — NC, AL → Rusk County, TX
Gen 6Alexander Howard Berry × Josephine Gregory — Texas
Gen 7Leona Berry × Ben Summers / Dick Simpson — Indian Territory → Borden County, TX

Generation 7 Leona Berry

Born
November 19, 1894, Indian Territory
Married
1st: Ben Summers (m. January 4, 1911; d. 1918) — 2nd: Montgomery 'Dick' Simpson (m. November 27, 1920)
Parents
Alexander Howard Berry and Josephine Priscilla Gregory Berry
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

Leona Berry was born on November 19, 1894 in Indian Territory. Leona Berry married Ben Summers Jan 4 1911. He died in the 1917 flu epidemic in Colorado. The family heard there was work in Colorado so the A.H. Berrys, the Willie Berrys with son Alec, daughter Hettie and a second son, Willie Howell Jr., born June 2, 1917, the Ben Summers family with three children, Newborn, born 1913, J.C., born 1915 and Maggie born 1917, the Hal Johnsons and seven children, Myrtle, Edith, Lula, Matthew, Albert, WillEtta, and Leola, and the C.L. Johnsons all loaded into covered wagons and went to Pueblo, Colorado. When they arrived at Pueblo, the Hal Johnson, and Ben Summers families loaded their belongings into boxcars and continued on to Rifle, Colorado. A.H. Berry was not well and decided to continue on in the wagons. He was accompanied by the Willie Berry family. The C.L. Johnson family stayed in Pueblo. Rifle was across the mountains from Pueblo and when A.H. and his group reached Salida, Colorado the altitude was too high and he could not breathe. They backed the wheels of the wagons into the river to turn around as the road was too narrow, and returned to Pueblo. When they arrived there was a message waiting for Willie from Hal Johnson asking them to wait as they were returning on the same train they had gone on. They had unloaded nothing but the horses. After they returned to Pueblo the group found jobs and went to work. In a few months A.H. became homesick and wanted to return to Texas. The A.H. Berrys and the Willie Berrys did so at that time, August 1918, but the Johnsons and Summers families did not return with them. Those who stayed in Colorado all came down with the influenza and Ben Summers died. So many people were dying that it was almost a week before he could be buried in Pueblo. A few months after Ben Summers death, Hal Johnson and his family and Leona (Onie) Berry Summers and her children loaded their livestock and belongings into a boxcar and rode the train back to O’Donnell. That was December 1918. When they returned to Texas they again settled in Borden County. Leona and her three children moved into the house with her parents.

On November 27, 1920 Leona (Onie) Summers married Montgomery (Dick) Simpson in Tahoka, Texas.

item.title: Mary A. Berry NC
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Mary A. Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3William Berry × Hannah Cate — Orange County, NC
Gen 4Thomas Person Berry × Sarah Lunsford — Orange County, NC
Gen 5John Robert Berry × Elizabeth Frances Bowling — Orange County, NC
Gen 6Mary A. Berry × James G. Holeman / James Ormond Webb — Orange County, NC

Generation 6 Mary A. Berry

Born
May 18, 1873
Died
May 23, 1963
Married
1st: James G. Holeman (married June 25, 1896) — 2nd: James Ormond Webb (married January 27, 1904)
Parents
John Robert Berry and Elizabeth Frances Bowling, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Mary A. Berry#

Mary W. Berry was born May 18, 1873 and died in May 23, 1963 (1900, 1910, 1920 Orange County Censuses). Mary W. Holeman is listed living in the home of her father John Robert Berry in 1900 Census. We have recently found that Mary W. Berry was first married to James G. Holeman on June 25th 1896. He was the son of James and Emma B. Holeman from Orange County, NC. Her second marriage was to James Ormond Webb on January 27th 1904. In the 1910 census she was listed as the wife of James O. Webb and they had a 5 year old son named James O. Webb Jr. James Ormond Webb Sr. was born in 1859 and died in June 19th 1936. His parents were Sidney Smith Webb and Elizabeth Ellen Morrow. Both Jim and Mary Berry Webb are buried at Bethleham Presbyterian Church cemetery in Orange County Cemetery, Orange County, NC. There were only two children born to this union.

Mary J Berry grew up in the Roswell Georgia area of Milton County Georgia. In 1930 Milton County Georgia was renamed Fulton County Georgia. Mary J. Berry married Nathan Turley born August 25, 1838 and died December 26, 1923. The picture on the right is of Nathan Turley. There are no pictures known to exist of Mary J. Berry. Click on the picture to view a larger image. Nathan and Mary J Berry Turley had four Children

item.title: Mary Polly Cates Georgia
item.description:
Parents: William Abraham Cates and Sarah Berry

Mary Polly Cates was born 10th January 1822 in South Carolina as her parents made their way to Georgia. She marries James Alexander Walton in 1848 shortly after his first wife died.

item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors

Born
c. 1873, Milton County, Georgia
Died
1937, buried in Ponder Cemetery
Married
1st: Mettie A. Haley (married November 25, 1900) — 2nd: Nancy B. Garrison (married January 6, 1926)
Parents
Nathan Turley and Mary Jori Berry
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

NATHANIEL ARIE TURLEY was born about 1873 in Milton County Georgia. He died in 1937 and was buried in Ponder Cemetery. Arie, as he was known by the family, was married twice. He married Mettie A. Haley on November 25, 1900. When Mettie died, Arie married Nancy B. Garrison on January 6, 1926. There were no children born to either of these unions.

item.title: Orange County NC Marriage Bonds A - C
item.description:
Orange County, NC Marriage Bonds — Surnames A through C

Indexed by groom. Includes 15 Berry family marriage bonds from 1797 to 1857.

File contributed for use in USGenWeb Archives by Betty Mayes. Check original bonds in Archives for additional information. Some spelling and dates may vary from the originals.

Died

Married
Felix Curry 'Bob' Wilson (married December 20, 1910)
Parents
John Robert Berry and Elizabeth Francis Bowling, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Pattie Swiney Berry#

PATTIE SWINEY BERRY was born on August 8, 1886, in Orange County, North Carolina. She

only lived for 29 years because she died on December 12, 1915, in Red Springs, Robeson County, NC. She is buried at Berry’s Grove Baptist Church Cemetery, Little River Township, Orange County, NC.

item.title: Robert Berry Joshua's Son NC
item.description:
Robert Berry, Joshua's Son

Son of Joshua and Nancy Ellison Berry, Orange County, North Carolina. Married Sally Cates.

Robert Berry, son of Joshua Berry and Nancy Ellison Berry#

Ancestors#

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County, Virginia.
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.
  3. Generation Joshua and Nancy Ellison Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.

ROBERT BERRY was born in 1800 in Orange County North Carolina. Robert Berry and Sally Cates were married in Person County North Carolina circa 1814. There is a great deal that we did not know about this family. We know that they did live in Orange County for their entire lives or at least until 1850. Sally Berry age 52 (born 1798) listed as head of household in 1850 with her two daughters Rachel Berry age 11 (born 1839) and Sarah Berry age 14 (born 1836).

item.title: Robert C. Berry NC Ala
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors

Generation 4 Robert C. Berry

Born
April 14, 1805, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
Fayette County (Berry), Alabama
Married
Sarah Chappell (married c. 1839, Fayette County, Alabama)
Parents
William Berry and Hannah Cate Berry, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

Son of William and Hannah Cate Berry#

Robert C. Berry was born on April 14th 1805 in Orange County, North Carolina. Robert C. did not marry in Orange County and was about 29 or 30 years old when he left the state. Since he was single and didn’t own property there are no official records naming him. He did however witness the sale of property on September 19th 1833 that his two brothers sold.

item.title: Robert Riley Berry Ga
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines

Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, NC → Georgia
Gen 4Robert Berry × Elizabeth Everitt — Milton County, Georgia
Gen 5Robert Riley Berry × Martha Stark — Milton County, GA → Cullman County, AL

Generation 5 Robert Riley Berry

Born
June 10, 1840, Fayette County, Georgia
Died
March 31, 1909, Vinemont, Cullman County, Alabama
Married
Martha 'Mattie' Stark
Parents
Robert and Elizabeth Everitt Berry
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Robert Riley Berry#

Robert Riley Berry was born June 10, 1840 in Fayette County, Georgia. View Robert’s birthday entry in the family bible page. Robert Riley Berry grew up in the Roswell, Georgia area of Milton County, Georgia. In 1930 Milton County, Georgia was renamed Fulton County, Georgia. Robert Riley and two of his brothers married three sisters. The young ladies were the daughters of Jonas and Averella Kent Stark. Robert Riley married Martha “Mattie” Stark. The picture on the left is of Robert Riley and Mattie Stark Berry. Click on the picture to view a larger image. The other two brothers were William H. Berry who married Matilda Stark and John Allmon Berry who married Sarah “Sally” Stark. Robert died on March 31, 1909 in Vinemont, Cullman County, Alabama, USA[efn_note]Find A Grave profile for Robert Riley Berry[/efn_note].

  2. Robert Berry and Elizabeth Cate Orange County NC
  3. Berry Unknown
  4. Robert T Berry (Susan Roberson is Robert T Berry’s mother)

Gen 6Thomas C. Guthrie × Mary Frances Crow
Gen 7Lonnie Joseph Hunt × Lela Guthrie
Gen 8Ruby Alyene Hunt × Robert Raymond Vinson — Minden, Rusk County, TX

Generation 8 Ruby Alyene Hunt

Born
July 22, 1916, Minden, Rusk County, Texas
Died
November 7, 1982, Henderson, Rusk County, Texas
Married
Robert Raymond Vinson (b. November 25, 1903; d. June 13, 1979)
Parents
Lela Guthrie Hunt and Lonnie Joseph Hunt
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

Ruby Alyene Hunt was born on July 22nd 1916 in Minden, Rusk County Texas and died on November 7th 1982 in Henderson, Rusk County Texas. She married Robert Raymond Vinson who was born on November 25th 1903 in New London, Rusk County Texas. Robert died on June 13th 1979 in Henderson, Rusk County Texas. They are buried in Pleasant Hill Cemetery between Henderson and Overton in Rusk County.

item.title: Sarah Berry (TX)
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Sarah Berry (TX)
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Robert Berry Jr. × Mary Waggoner — Orange County, NC → Fayette County, AL
Gen 4George Berry × Susan Berry — NC → Fayette County, AL
Gen 5John Berry × Mary Howell — NC, AL → Rusk County, TX
Gen 6Sarah Berry × Hiram Howard / Francis McClure / Robert Taylor / Henry Stoneham / Henrick Dippold — Alabama → Rusk County, TX → Galveston, TX

Generation 6 Sarah Berry

Born
April 20, 1839, Alabama
Died
June 5, 1917, Galveston, Texas
Married
1st: Hiram Howard (m. 1853) — 2nd: Francis M. McClure (m. 1859) — 3rd: Robert A. Taylor (m. 1863) — 4th: Henry B. Stoneham (m. 1868) — 5th: Henrick Dippold (m. 1880)
Parents
John Berry and Mary Howell Berry
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

She was born on April 20, 1839 in Alabama and died in Galveston Texas on June 5th 1917. We were able to find Sarah Berry Dippold’s grave in 2006 while I was staying in Houston for medical reasons. The grave is located in the Trinity Episcopal Church Cemetery which is located in Galveston Texas. She is buried in Block # 100 plot 18 of that Cemetery. I was unable to locate her husband but he may still be buried there. This Cemetery has been raised several times, as it keeps sinking they haul in more dirt. Each family is responsible to see that their relatives headstones are raised to the new level. If the family does not assume that responsibility the grave is covered with dirt and is no longer visible. The sinking of the Island is caused by removal of oil from the surface below. Sarah was married five times. This was during the period of the civil war and may be the reason so many of them died. She married her first husband Hiram Howard on November 24th 1853. Sarah married her second husband, Francis M. McClure on January 5th 1859. On May 18th 1863 Sarah married her third husband Robert A. Taylor. Sarah married her fourth husband, Henry B. Stoneham on August 29 1868. She married her fifth husband Henrick Dippold on February 15th 1880.

item.title: Sarah Frances Berry Kelly GA
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines

Born
Georgia
Died
1954
Married
James Martin Kelly
Parents
Jesse Lee Berry and Nancy Henry Berry
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

L to R. Sarah Florence Berry Kelly, William Oscar Berry, Albert Edgar Berry, Addie Lena Berry Maners, Cora Mae Berry McVickers, Walter Glenn Berry is not shown in this picture

item.title: Tennessee Picture Album
item.description:
Tennessee Picture Album

Photographs of the Thomas and Sarah Cate Berry family in Tennessee.

Pictures for Thomas And Sarah Cate Berry Family#

Ancestors:

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry, Princess Anne County, Virginia.
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry, Orange County, North Carolina.
  3. Generation Thomas and Sarah Cate Berry Orange County NC & Wayne County Tennessee

DAVID II BERRY was born about 1802 and was Thomas and Sarah Berry’s only son. David was about six years when he, his sister Eleanor and his mother Sarah moved in with his aunt Hannah. David’s first cousin Thomas Person Berry was a baby when David moved in but David lived with his aunt Hannah and her children from about 1808 until about 1815. Both husbands had gone to the west in search of land. Thomas apparently was killed by Indians and Sarah was forced to start a new life for herself and her children. David was about thirteen years old when his mother married John Pigg and removed to Tennessee. David lived the rest of his life in Tennessee and for a short time worked in Alabama. He married Elizabeth Shipman about 1826. They had fourteen children comprising of eight boys and six girls. David Berry and Elizabeth both died around 1882 and are buried in the Jackson Cemetery in Wayne County at Cypress Inn Tennessee

item.title: Walter Glen Berry Ga
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines

Gen 4Robert Berry × Elizabeth Everitt — Milton County, Georgia
Gen 5Jesse Lee Berry × Nancy Ann Henry — Georgia → Alabama
Gen 6Walter Glen Berry × Leone Paschall Floyd — Etowah County, AL → Henry County, GA

Generation 6 Walter Glen Berry

Born
January 15, 1880, Etowah County, Alabama
Died
May 2, 1951, Henry County, Georgia
Married
Leone Paschall Floyd (married December 22, 1901; born October 24, 1881; died October 10, 1966)
Parents
Jesse Lee and Nancy Ann Henry Berry
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Walter Glen Berry#

L to R. Sarah Florence Berry Kelly, William Oscar Berry, Albert Edgar Berry, Addie Lena Berry Maners, Cora Mae Berry McVickers, Walter Glenn Berry is not shown in this picture

item.title: Wiley Harold Berry NC
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Wiley Harold Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3William Berry × Hannah Cate — Orange County, NC
Gen 4Thomas Person Berry × Sarah Lunsford — Orange County, NC
Gen 5John Robert Berry × Elizabeth Bowling — Orange County, NC
Gen 6Wiley P. Berry × Ida Ann Wilson — Orange County, NC
Gen 7Wiley Harold Berry × Hilda Elizabeth Nichols — Orange County, NC → Durham, NC

Generation 7 Wiley Harold Berry

Born
March 9, 1914, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
June 24, 2006, Durham, North Carolina
Married
Hilda Elizabeth Nichols (married December 27, 1934)
Parents
Wiley P. Berry Sr. and Ida Ann Wilson Berry, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Wiley Harold Berry#

This is probably the first picture ever taken of Wiley Harold Berry. He is the baby on the left of his sister and my mother. My Mother, Sally Malena Berry, was born in 1908 which would make her six years old in 1914. Wiley Harold Berry was born on March 9th 1914. Woodrow Wilson Berry is on the right of his sister. The picture was taken on the south east side of my grandparents back porch and you can see the well in the background. The porch has a wooden floor in this picture. This floor was made of concrete before I was old enough to remember anything about it. I took the color picture below at a family reunion in 1988 shortly before Woodrow died. The interesting thing between the two pictures is the direction Harold, Mother, and Woodrow were looking in both pictures. The picture was not posed with this in mind. It was one of the little coincidences that just happened. The reunion was held at Harold’s oldest daughter’s home in Durham, North Carolina. There was a little fish pond on the property and Woodrow was doing a little fishing.

item.title: William Clarence Berry NC, ALA, TX
item.description:
William Clarence Berry (1803–1877)

Son of William and Hannah Cate Berry, from Orange County NC to Fayette County Alabama and Burnet County Texas.

item.title: William Thomas Berry Sr.
item.description:
William Thomas Berry Sr.

Son of Henry Bradford Berry and Malissa Jeffries Berry, Alabama.

William Thomas Berry Sr., son of Henry Bradford Berry and Malissa Jeffries#

ANCESTORS

Generation 1 Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry

Generation 2 Robert Jr and Mary Camp Berry

item.title: Willie Adolphus Berry NC
item.description:
Willie Adolphus Berry (1877–1961)

Son of John Robert and Elizabeth Frances Bowling Berry, Orange County, North Carolina.

Willie Adolphus Berry, was the son of John Robert Berry Orange County, NC#

ANCESTORS

Generation 1 Robert and Mary Williamson Berry

Generation 2 Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry
